title: Helictochloa × talaverae
intro: Helictochloa × talaverae, also known as Spanish bent or Talavera bentgrass, is a hybrid grass species that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the reduction of inflammation and the promotion of antioxidant activity, which can contribute to overall well-being. Helictochloa × talaverae contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Helictochloa × talaverae include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms associated with anxiety and insomnia.
taxonomy: Helictochloa × talaverae is a hybrid species of grass within the family Poaceae. It is believed to be a cross between Helictochloa pallescens and a species of the genus Helictochloa. The classification of Helictochloa × talaverae is still under research and not well-documented in botanical literature. Further study is necessary to determine the exact classification of this hybrid species. Helictochloa × talaverae is likely to be classified as a hybrid within the genus Helictochloa.
